headline: Certificate linting arrives as pki.lint -- graded, advisory conformance findings against the RFC 5280 profile and a representative CA/Browser Forum TLS Baseline Requirements subset.
summary: pki.lint is the zlint / pkilint of JavaScript: it walks a parsed certificate and emits graded advisory findings -- each with a stable id, a severity (fatal > error > warn > notice), a source, a spec-clause citation, and a human message -- rather than a single pass/fail verdict, because a certificate can be perfectly valid DER yet violate many profile rules. certificate(input, opts) accepts a PEM string, a DER Buffer, or an already-parsed certificate; rules(profile) and profiles() enumerate the registry. Unlike every other entry in the toolkit the lint DATA path never throws: hostile or truncated bytes return a report whose worst finding is a fatal lint/unparseable carrying the strict parser's own error code, so an operator can lint an entire directory without a try/catch around each file. The sole throw path is config-time misuse -- an unknown profile, an out-of-range severity threshold, or a wrong-type input -- which raises a typed LintError. v1 ships the RFC 5280 certificate profile (serial sign and octet length, validity ordering, CA keyCertSign coherence, unknown critical extensions, empty-subject SAN criticality, and SKI/AKI presence advisories) plus a representative CABF TLS BR subset (subjectAltName required, commonName must also appear as a dNSName, dNSName syntax, serverAuth EKU, weak keys below RSA-2048 or off an approved curve, and the reducing validity ceiling gated to the certificate's issuance date -- 398 days, then 200 from 2026-03-15, 100 from 2027-03-15, and 47 from 2029-03-15 per Ballot SC081v3). Because this toolkit's parser is strict-DER, several profile violations a lenient linter reports as a specific finding are instead rejected at parse and surface here as one fatal lint/unparseable whose context names the exact violation.

Added: pki.lint.certificate(input, opts) lints a certificate against the RFC 5280 profile and a representative CA/Browser Forum TLS BR subset, returning a report of graded advisory findings (id, severity, source, spec citation, message). The data path never throws -- malformed input becomes a fatal lint/unparseable finding rather than an exception -- so a corpus lints without per-file error handling; only config-time misuse raises a typed LintError.
pki.lint.rules(profile) and pki.lint.profiles() enumerate the rule registry and the available profiles (rfc5280, cabf-tls) so findings are traceable to a stable id and a spec clause.
